perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may not make additional copies of the confidential information without the Disclosing Party's prior consent to be given on a c ase by case basis.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may not make additional copies of the restricted information without the Disclosing Party's prior consent to be given on a c ase by case basis.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Recipient' and 'confidential information' to 'restricted information' introduces uncertainty about which entity and which information the clause applies to. If 'Recipient' isn't the same as 'Receiving Party', and 'restricted information' isn't the same as 'Confidential Information,' then Section 6, which states 'The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and ev ery term and condition set forth herein', is directly contradicted. The Receiving Party can argue they are not obligated by this clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Abidance to the terms and conditions of this agr eement The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and ev ery term and condition set forth herein. Each party acknowledges that each of such terms and cond itions is necessary to preserve the confidentiality of the information provided to it a nd that a breach of any of the terms and conditions hereof will result in irreparable damage to the Dis closing Party /parties in an amount now impossible to calculate and, thereof, in the event of such breach, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled (in an addition to any other rights and r emedies it may have at law or in equity) to have an injunction issued by any court of competent jurisdi ction enjoining it and any other person involved therein from communing such breach.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Recipient' and 'confidential information' to 'restricted information' creates a strong in-text contradiction. Section 6 explicitly states, 'The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and every term and condition set forth herein.' If 'Recipient' and 'Receiving Party' are not the same, and 'restricted information' and 'confidential information' are not the same, then the Receiving Party could argue they are not bound by the clause in the Changed Text. This directly contradicts the original intention of Section 6, causing ambiguity and potential unenforceability, because it's no longer certain who or what information the clause applies to. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party make any warranties, e xpress or implied, regarding the accuracy, completeness, performance, merchantability, fitness for use, non:infringement of third parties, rights, or other attributes of its respective confi dential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party makes any guarantees, express or implied, regarding the precision, comprehensiveness, efficacy, marketability, suitability for application, non-violation of third-party rights, or other characteristics of its corresponding privileged data.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation replaces numerous terms: 'warranties' becomes 'guarantees,' 'accuracy' becomes 'precision,' 'completeness' becomes 'comprehensiveness,' 'performance' becomes 'efficacy,' 'merchantability' becomes 'marketability,' 'fitness for use' becomes 'suitability for application,' 'non-infringement' becomes 'non-violation,' 'rights' becomes 'third-party rights', and 'confidential information' becomes 'privileged data.' The term 'privileged data' is undefined. It is now unclear if the disclaimer in Section 4 applies to 'Confidential Information' as defined in Section 1, creating ambiguity and potential conflict. This contradicts Section 5, which refers to 'Confidential Information' in the context of destruction or return upon withdrawal from the agreement, and raises questions if privileged data applies to the same condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Withdrawal from the agreement Immediately upon the decision by either party not t o enter into the transaction or agreement contemplated by the parties hereto, and based on th e Disclosing Party's written request, the Receiving Party shall either destroy and certify su ch destruction or return to the Disclosing Party al l copies or extracts of the Disclosing Party’s Confid ential Information, in any medium and destruct it. Such complete return or destruction will be promptl y and duly confirmed in writing to the Disclosing Party or its proxy by the Receiving Part y.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'privileged data,' which is undefined. This creates a strong contradiction with the contradicted text that specifies the procedure for destruction or return of 'Confidential Information' upon withdrawal from the agreement. Since 'privileged data' is undefined, it's unclear if the destruction/return obligation applies to it, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability of the original agreement regarding 'Confidential Information' as laid out in the contradicted text. This is an instance of Misaligned Terminology leading to an in-text contradiction, as per the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to Confidential Information received prior to termination, will survive for a period of three (3) years following the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Accepting Party with respect to Sensitive Data received prior to termination, will survive for a period of one (1) years following the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Accepting Party', 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' and the survival period from three (3) years to one (1) year introduces several contradictions. First, if 'Accepting Party' is not the same as 'Receiving Party', and 'Sensitive Data' is not the same as 'Confidential Information', then it becomes unclear which party and which information are bound by the original NDA. Furthermore, The time period directly contradicts the defined period, which can lead to dispute of enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Abidance to the terms and conditions of this agr eement The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and ev ery term and condition set forth herein. Each party acknowledges that each of such terms and cond itions is necessary to preserve the confidentiality of the information provided to it a nd that a breach of any of the terms and conditions hereof will result in irreparable damage to the Dis closing Party /parties in an amount now impossible to calculate and, thereof, in the event of such breach, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled (in an addition to any other rights and r emedies it may have at law or in equity) to have an injunction issued by any court of competent jurisdi ction enjoining it and any other person involved therein from communing such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters key terms ('Receiving Party' to 'Accepting Party', 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data') and the survival period from three (3) years to one (1) year, creating a direct conflict with the contradicted section. The contradicted text explicitly states "The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and ev ery term and condition set forth herein." By changing "Receiving Party" to "Accepting Party," the obligation of the 'Receiving Party' as stated in the Contradicted Text is no longer clear. Also, the change introduces a different time frame for the survival of obligations (one year versus three years), which is a clear inconsistency as defined in the instructions. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the use of multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior unde rstanding and agreements between and among them respecting the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement comprises the complete understanding among the entities concerning the matters herein and substitutes any earlier arrangements and accords among them about the concerns herein.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation replaces several key terms: 'constitutes' becomes 'comprises,' 'entire agreement' becomes 'complete understanding,' 'parties' becomes 'entities,' 'subject matter' becomes 'matters,' 'supersedes' becomes 'substitutes,' 'prior understanding and agreements' becomes 'earlier arrangements and accords,' and 'respecting the subject matter' becomes 'about the concerns.' Using these new, undefined terms can create legal ambiguity. Does 'complete understanding' mean the same thing as 'entire agreement' in terms of excluding prior agreements or side deals? Does 'entities' include subsidiaries or affiliates, which the original 'parties' may not have? This casts doubt on the agreement's enforceability and scope as it contradicts the clarity sought in Section 1, where 'Disclosing Party' explicitly includes subsidiaries.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement is entered into as of ______________ (the “ Effective Date ”) by and between, Adv. Ofer Shapira and Adv. Oded Goldstein As receivers o n the "Pledged Assets" as defined in Tel:Aviv district court's resolution dated 2.12.10 (" The Receivers " and), at 1 Azrieli Center, Tel:Aviv, Israel, including all affiliated and subsidiaries companies being part of the Pledged Assets on the one hand (the “Disclosing Party” ) and _____________________________, on the other ( the “Receiving Party” ). In consideration of the mutual promises and covenan ts contained in this Agreement, the parties hereto agree as follows: 1. Confidential Information For the purpose of this Agreement “ Confidential Information ” means any and all information, disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, whether in oral or in written form, including but not limited to documentation, technic al or business information, ideas, plans, drawings, models and rights:of:way, requirements, s tandards, financial and non:financial data, marketing, trade secrets, know:how, customer and t enants lists, prices, as well as any and all intellectual and industrial property rights contain ed therein and/or in relation thereto; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not in clude information which (i) is or becomes lawfully in the public domain other than through a breach of any non disclosure agreement or any confidentiality obligation, (ii) was known to the Receiving Party prior to the disclosure, as evidenced by it, (iii) was independently develope d by or for the Receiving Party without reference to or use of Confidential Information received from the Disclosing Party, (iv) was lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party without v iolation of a confidentiality obligation, (v) The Disclosing Party agrees in writing that it may be d isclosed by the Receiving Party, or (vi) is require d or compelled by law to be disclosed, provided that the Receiving Party gives reasonable prior written notice to the Disclosing Party to allow it to seek protective or other court orders.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces key legal terms like 'entire agreement' with 'complete understanding' and 'parties' with 'entities'. This creates ambiguity as these new terms are undefined within the contract. The original text provides a clearer definition of 'Disclosing Party' that explicitly includes subsidiaries and affiliated companies. However, the changed text introduces the term 'entities' which may or may not include these. Since the 'Disclosing Party' definition from the original document (present in the contradicted text) is now potentially at odds with the term 'entities' found in the changed text, a contradiction arises concerning the scope of parties covered by the agreement. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction perturbation type, where the undefined terms create conflicting obligations.
